title: Set Up a Chart of Accounts and Process Basic Transactions
tagline: Configure a customized Chart of Accounts and record double-entry transactions for a freelancer.
scenario: A freelance graphic designer wants to move from Excel spreadsheets to cloud accounting software. They need a custom Chart of Accounts set up and their first month of business transactions processed.
brief: Create a custom Chart of Accounts tailored for a service-based freelancer. Record a list of 10 initial transactions (including capital injection, equipment purchase, client invoicing, and bank expenses) using double-entry ledger format (debits and credits).
deliverables: Custom Chart of Accounts list categorized by Assets, Liabilities, Equity, Revenue, and Expenses.
General Journal entries for the 10 listed transactions with correct debit/credit allocations.
A simple initial Trial Balance as of the end of the month.
submissionGuidance: Format your journal entries in clear tables. Ensure all debits equal credits and explain the ledger mapping logic.

rubric.label: Chart of Accounts Design
rubric.description: Is the Chart of Accounts logically organized and tailored for a freelancer?

rubric.label: Double-Entry Accuracy
rubric.description: Are debits and credits mathematically correct and conceptually accurate for all 10 transactions?

rubric.label: Trial Balance Integrity
rubric.description: Does the Trial Balance balance correctly and trace accurately from the journal entries?

rubric.label: Bookkeeping Notes
rubric.description: Are the explanation notes clear, professional, and explain basic accounting concepts well?
